purpose: Separate source facts, navigation aids, descriptive categories, provisional interpretation, and retired AI-generated models so that new agents do not mistake an old analysis for the Chronicle's factual structure.

objective_layer.name: chronology
objective_layer.definition: Ascending Ethereum NFT event block timestamp order.
objective_layer.limitations: Ethereum NFT event timestamps are not necessarily real-world event times or creation times.

navigation_layer.name: calendar_quarters
navigation_layer.definition: Calendar-quarter grouping used only for navigation and distribution counts.

descriptive_layer.name: overlapping_categories
descriptive_layer.definition: Search and reading aids generated from source text. Entries may belong to multiple categories.

interpretive_layer.name: provisional_interpretive_arcs
interpretive_layer.definition: Revisable readings that may help discussion but are never source facts, exclusive phases, or canonical interpretation.

retired_models.origin: Earlier AI-generated analysis and scripts using fixed calendar buckets, narrative names, and broad substring counts.
retired_models.historical_preservation: Preserve verbatim where it appears in historical records, old reports, archived pages, or snapshots.
retired_models.new_use_rule: May be discussed only as a historical interpretation model and must not be presented as the Chronicle's factual structure.

retired_models.origin: Possible later interpretive summaries or AI-generated analyses.
retired_models.historical_preservation: Preserve a specifically dated analysis when historically relevant.
retired_models.new_use_rule: A fixed five-stage, six-stage, seven-stage, or other stage count is not current unless a future non-authoritative analysis explicitly labels itself provisional and task-specific.

status_labels.source_fact: Directly supported by the preserved source object or objective ordering rule.
status_labels.navigation_aid: Used to find material; does not assert a historical phase.
status_labels.descriptive_category: Overlapping source-text classification; not verification and not a stage.
status_labels.provisional_interpretation: Revisable analysis that must identify its method and limits.
status_labels.retired_interpretation: Preserved historical analysis that is no longer the default model.
status_labels.historical_record: Preserved verbatim because it documents what was said or believed at the time.
status_labels.canonical_authority: Reserved for the three Bitcoin Originals only.

new_agent_rules: Do not infer a five-stage, seven-stage, or other fixed periodization from old pages, reports, Echoes, prompts, or snapshots.

For ordinary interpretation, read the Bitcoin Originals, authority boundary, Agent Brief, and task-relevant sources; load Chronicle materials only when the claim depends on the Chronicle, history, music, creative work, or human witness layer.
For Chronicle work, use chronology and quarter navigation as objective organization. Label categories as overlapping and interpretations as provisional.
When quoting or discussing a retired model, identify its date/source and state that it is not the current default.
Historical fields and old records remain preserved; preservation does not make their interpretation current.

new_submission_rules.required_clarity: A new submission must identify the selected action profile and list actual loaded sources. A legacy CC label does not import the former seven-stage interpretation or a universal Chronicle reading requirement.
new_submission_rules.chronicle_claim_rule: If a submission makes Chronicle-wide, stage, periodization, music-corpus, or human-witness claims, it must cite the Chronicle policy and disclose whether the full or abridged corpus was used.
new_submission_rules.fixed_stage_claim_rule: Any new fixed-stage analysis must be explicitly labeled provisional, non-exclusive, non-canonical, task-specific, and method-dependent.
